text.title: Idea Generation Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Mode-collapse antipatterns, agent bias patterns specific to the 7P and PG methods, and mandatory human-in-loop checkpoints.

antipattern.description: LLMs default to "AI scheduler", "task tracker", "AI for X" outputs under default temperature settings.
antipattern.reason: Training data for solopreneur ideation is dominated by these patterns. Without explicit diversity forcing, agents repeat them across every framework and every session.

rule.statement: Set temperature to 0.8-0.9 for divergent ideation agents. Forbid "AI-powered", "smart", "intelligent", "GPT" as name prefixes in the prompt to force diverse framing.
rule.rationale: These terms are statistical attractors. Explicitly prohibiting them in the prompt forces the model to find alternative framings that expose different parts of the idea space.

antipattern.description: Agents conflate "passion" ideas with "profession" ideas, producing near-duplicate candidates across two P categories.
antipattern.reason: Without a strict definition prompt-side, the model treats passion and profession as semantically overlapping domains.

antipattern.description: PG question 3 ("what do you find yourself building for yourself") produces Dropbox/Notion clones without founder-specific personal context.
antipattern.reason: The model defaults to well-known successful answers from training data when personal context is absent.

rule.statement: Provide concrete founder context before running PG question 3: tools actually built, workflows automated, scripts written in the past 6 months. Generic profile → generic output.
rule.rationale: This question requires specific personal context to produce non-generic results. Without it, the agent falls back to pattern-matching against training data success stories.

list.item: Checkpoint 1: Founder profile accuracy before running ideation — verify skills, constraints, and daily routine are correctly captured.
Checkpoint 2: Shortlist review after generation, before scoring — founder reduces 35 ideas to 10-15 by gut before scoring begins.
Checkpoint 3: Top-3 sanity check before proceeding to niche-evaluation — founder confirms the three candidates are genuinely interesting, not agent-rationalized.
